reason: arena-tag renders only a tone dot (a plain <span>, no role) and projected content -- there is no output, no button, and no onRemove-equivalent input anywhere in the file. A user can look at the tag but has no way to remove it. React's Tag.jsx conditionally renders a real <button> for removal when its `onRemove` prop is passed; Angular's tag carries no removable variant at all, so this is a real capability gap, not an equivalent implementation with different DOM.
